summary: A runtime test failure in the Tcl tests: two tests in test.test_tcl failed because the Tcl result contained mojibake (UTF-8 bytes mis-decoded) instead of the expected Unicode surrogate escape representation.
root_cause: test.test_tcl.TclTest tests failed asserting that Tcl results preserve surrogate escapes; the test expected '<\U0001f4bb>' but got '<ðŸ’»>', indicating the Tcl result was decoded incorrectly (mojibake) on Windows.

evidence.reason: Shows that the failing test file is test_tcl.
Identifies the specific failing test name and that it failed.
Identifies the second failing test name and that it failed.
Points to the test file and the failing test location.
Shows the exact assertion failure: expected surrogate-escaped Unicode '<\U0001f4bb>' but got mojibake '<ðŸ’»>'.
Shows the Tcl version involved, which may be relevant to encoding/decoding behavior.

suggested_fix: Investigate Tcl-to-Python decoding on Windows: reproduce the failures by running the two tests in Lib\test\test_tcl.py and inspect the raw bytes returned by tcl.eval/tcl.evalfile. Verify that the Tcl library (patchlevel 9.0.3) and the Python Tcl wrapper decode results with UTF-8 (or use surrogate escapes) instead of producing mojibake; adjust the Tcl build/encoding settings or the Python wrapper's decoding logic so the tests receive '<\U0001f4bb>' as expected.
